input.prompt:
Locked camera, full-frame view of a hand-drawn pencil and watercolor portrait of a young man in his late teens with a wide smile on cream sketchbook paper.

PAGE-TURN motion — turning to the next blank page in a sketchbook:

[0.0 – 0.7s]: Hold on the late-teen portrait.

[0.7 – 1.5s]: The right edge of the page begins to LIFT and curl gently upward. The portrait starts to peel away from the right side.

[1.5 – 3.0s]: The page TURNS — the entire portrait page lifts up and rotates over toward the left, sliding out of the frame. As the page lifts, the BLANK CREAM PAGE underneath is revealed.

[3.0 – 4.5s]: The previous page finishes turning out of frame. The blank cream sketchbook page settles flat into view, completely empty. Same cream paper tone as before.

[4.5 – 5.0s]: Hold on the blank cream page.

Critical: real PAGE-TURN motion — paper physically lifting, curling, rotating away. NOT a fade. NOT a dissolve. Real paper physics.

Constraints: locked camera, no zoom, no pan. NO visible hand. Single continuous take. The clip MUST end exactly at the provided end frame.
